steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The sentence's subject is “The village of Panduyacu in Ecuador.” Although a prepositional phrase immediately follows the noun, such phrases modify the head noun without changing its number. “Village” is singular.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: Subject-verb agreement requires a singular subject to take a singular verb. Is is the singular present-tense form of be. Choices A and D both use the plural form are; choice B uses have been, which is plural present perfect. That matches choice C — C is correct.
